evals.prompt: I want to build an AI feature for our SaaS product where users can ask questions and get answers from our help center docs. We have 800 help articles but no labeled data and no existing code. Where do I start and in what order should I build this?
evals.expected_output: A phased plan starting with /ai-searching-docs as Phase 1 (build search and RAG pipeline), identifying the absence of labeled Q&A pairs as a data gap to address, followed by /ai-stopping-hallucinations or /ai-improving-accuracy in Phase 2, and /ai-serving-apis in a later phase. The plan uses the Goal/Skill/Deliverable template and limits the core path to 3-4 skills.

assertions.name: plan_starts_with_ai_searching_docs
assertions.description: Phase 1 references /ai-searching-docs as the skill for the search and RAG capability, not a generic description

assertions.name: identifies_data_gap
assertions.description: Plan explicitly flags the lack of labeled Q&A examples as something to address — does not skip the data question

assertions.name: uses_phased_template
assertions.description: Output is structured as named phases each with a Goal, Skill, and Deliverable — not a flat task list

assertions.name: limits_to_core_path
assertions.description: Plan recommends no more than 5 skills and defers non-critical capabilities to a skip-for-now section

evals.prompt: We have a support ticket classifier that we built last month. It works okay — maybe 70% accuracy — but we need to get it to production and improve it. What order should we tackle the remaining work?
evals.expected_output: A plan that starts with measuring current quality before optimizing (not jumping straight to optimization), uses /ai-improving-accuracy for evaluation and optimizer runs, then /ai-serving-apis for production deployment. The plan warns against running a DSPy optimizer before a baseline metric and eval set exist.

assertions.name: starts_with_measuring_not_optimizing
assertions.description: Phase 1 is establishing a metric and eval set, not immediately running an optimizer — skill matches the 'working prototype' starting point

assertions.name: references_ai_improving_accuracy
assertions.description: Plan uses /ai-improving-accuracy for the evaluate-and-optimize phase, not a generic suggestion to improve the prompt

assertions.name: mentions_eval_data_need
assertions.description: Plan surfaces the need for labeled evaluation examples before optimization can begin

evals.prompt: I need to build an invoice processing system. It should read PDF invoices, extract vendor name, invoice number, date, line items, and total, validate that the math checks out, and submit clean invoices to our ERP via REST API. What skills do I need and in what order?
evals.expected_output: A multi-phase plan mapping: /ai-parsing-data for PDF extraction (Phase 1), /ai-checking-outputs for math validation (Phase 2), /ai-building-pipelines to chain extract + validate + submit (Phase 3), and /ai-improving-accuracy for quality optimization (later). Dependencies are stated explicitly: validation requires extraction to work first; ERP submission comes only after validation is in place.

assertions.name: maps_to_ai_parsing_data
assertions.description: Plan uses /ai-parsing-data for PDF field extraction — not a generic code suggestion

assertions.name: maps_to_ai_checking_outputs
assertions.description: Plan uses /ai-checking-outputs for the math validation and business rule checking step

assertions.name: correct_dependency_order
assertions.description: Phase ordering puts extraction before validation, and validation before ERP submission — not all in parallel

assertions.name: states_phase_dependencies
assertions.description: Plan includes a Dependencies section that explains why each phase requires the previous phase output
